title: PenPens species
wikitext:
[]: PenPen's species             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Nov 15, 2010 9:42 am Does anyone know if he is a Fiordiland or a Snares? I'm working on something for school.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 15, 2010 9:51 am According to the wikipedia entry he's an erect-crested penguin but the article doesn't mention any source. I'd say that the more evident difference between Pen Pen and the real world Eudyptes (crested penguins) is that his crest is red rather than yellow and his eyes are green (or something similar) rather than red. EDIT: and of course there are his "fingers"; actually he barely looks like an erect-crested penguin (yet I putted Gunbuster in one power point presentation, I think that you can put Pen^2 without a problem)

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:35 am I almost want to say he's a bit of that and a bit of a Rockhopper too. Why? There's a hand-puppet of a Rockhopper I've been meaning to buy. It looks alarmingly like Pen-Pen, what with those magnificent beetle-brows.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:39 am Isnt he specifically referred to as a 'hot-springs penguin' within the show? Not sure if thats supposed to be the name of a species, be it real or fictional.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:53 am Jayfive wrote: Isnt he specifically referred to as a 'hot-springs penguin' within the show? Not sure if thats supposed to be the name of a species, be it real or fictional.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 15, 2010 1:54 pm Im going with some influence from: http://home.sjfc.edu/cals/units/mcgowan/snares_crested.jpg Snares Crested Penguin (eudyptes robustus). But with the red and yellow colouring on the head switched.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Nov 15, 2010 3:16 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Yeah, but since that species is fictional one could want to understand which real species Sadamoto used as inspiration. I wonder if he ever said something about it.

Article: PenPens species
[]: PenPen's species             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Nov 15, 2010 9:42 am Does anyone know if he is a Fiordiland or a Snares? I'm working on something for school.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 15, 2010 9:51 am According to the wikipedia entry he's an erect-crested penguin but the article doesn't mention any source. I'd say that the more evident difference between Pen Pen and the real world Eudyptes (crested penguins) is that his crest is red rather than yellow and his eyes are green (or something similar) rather than red. EDIT: and of course there are his "fingers"; actually he barely looks like an erect-crested penguin (yet I putted Gunbuster in one power point presentation, I think that you can put Pen^2 without a problem)

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:35 am I almost want to say he's a bit of that and a bit of a Rockhopper too. Why? There's a hand-puppet of a Rockhopper I've been meaning to buy. It looks alarmingly like Pen-Pen, what with those magnificent beetle-brows.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:39 am Isnt he specifically referred to as a 'hot-springs penguin' within the show? Not sure if thats supposed to be the name of a species, be it real or fictional.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Nov 15, 2010 11:53 am Jayfive wrote: Isnt he specifically referred to as a 'hot-springs penguin' within the show? Not sure if thats supposed to be the name of a species, be it real or fictional.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 15, 2010 1:54 pm Im going with some influence from: http://home.sjfc.edu/cals/units/mcgowan/snares_crested.jpg Snares Crested Penguin (eudyptes robustus). But with the red and yellow colouring on the head switched.
